title: Responsive Design with Frameworks Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of the mobile-first approach, breakpoints (sm/md/lg), and Flexbox/Grid for cross-device consistency.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primarily design philosophy for modern frontend frameworks like Bootstrap and Tailwind CSS?
Which and/or what is a 'breakpoint' in the context of responsive design?
options: A point where the JavaScript code breaks.
A specific screen width (pixel value) where a website's layout or styles will change.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.

questions.question: Which prefix would you use in Tailwind to apply a style only on screens larger than 'medium'?
Does using a 12-column grid system (like in Bootstrap) allow for fluid and responsive layouts?
options: Yes, as columns can span different widths on different screen sizes using responsive classes.

questions.question: Which CSS layout technologies are used under the hood by most modern frameworks to handle responsiveness?
